message:
fix(codegen): Handle 'try' at module level and improve variable capture

Multiple fixes for codegen issues:

1. Module-level 'try' handling:
   - type_checks.zig: Use 'catch unreachable' instead of 'try' for
     dynamicType() calls at module level
   - generator.zig: Wrap module-level tuple unpacking function calls
     with 'catch unreachable'

2. Variable capture fixes:
   - expressions.zig: Add checks for closure name patterns (_c_ and
     _closure_) in var_renames lookup
   - function_gen.zig: Preserve var_renames when generating nested
     class method bodies (save/restore pattern)
   - var_tracking.zig: Check subscript indices in assignment targets
     for parameter usage detection (fixes 'i' undeclared in b[i]=val)

These fixes address compilation errors where:
- 'try' was used outside function scope in Zig
- Nested function references were losing their renamed identifiers
- Zero-capture closures weren't detecting outer variable usage
